title: Roasted and Spicy Croatian Casserole
description: A hearty, croatian dish featuring hotsauce and infuse-oil, perfect for a cozy night in. Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley.

ingredients.name: infuse oil
ingredients.notes: olive oil infused with garlic and chili peppers,

instructions: In a large skillet, heat the infuse oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the ground caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 20-25 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through.
While the pork is cooking, mix the hotsauce and smoked paprika in a small bowl.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the hotsauce-paprika mixture evenly over the top of the pork.
Transfer the pork to a 9x13 inch baking dish and top with the shredded cheddar cheese.
In a small bowl, mix the all-purpose flour and chicken broth to make a roux. Pour the roux over the top of the cheese.
Return the baking dish to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the baking dish from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
